channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FirstBank rolls out Visa Signature card for Nigeria's high-end customers
item.description: First Bank of Nigeria Limited don join hand with Visa to launch Visa Signature, one kain premium card wey dem design specifically for Nigeria's wealthy people dem. This card go give holders plenty ben...

item.title: Insights and Techniques for Creating Compelling Portraits with Todd Hido
item.description: In this in-depth interview, acclaimed American photographer Todd Hido shares his insights on creating impactful photographic portraits. Hido emphasizes that the power of a portrait comes not only from...

item.title: Police Arrest Two Men Over Unlicensed Firearms Seen in Viral Video
item.description: Police in South Africa have arrested two men after a video circulated on social media showing women recklessly handling firearms. The footage, which went viral over the weekend, depicts two women poin...

item.title: Barrick and Government Continue $30 Million Project to Improve Schools in Tanzania
item.description: The second phase of the Barrick-Twiga Future Forward Education Programme in Tanzania is now 83 percent done. This $30 million initiative, jointly run by the government and Barrick Mining Corporation, ...

item.title: UAE introduces fixed monthly deadline for private-sector salary payments
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has announced a new regulation to standardise salary payments for private-sector employees under the Wage Protection System (WPS). Starting June 1, 2026, all private companies...

item.title: Prince William plans to sell a fifth of Duchy of Cornwall holdings to fund housing and environmental initiatives
item.description: The Duchy of Cornwall, which provides Prince William with a private income exceeding £20 million per year, has announced plans to sell 20% of its property portfolio over the next decade. The estimated...

item.title: DOE Announces Gasoline and Diesel Price Increase Amid Slight Drop in Fuel Inventory
item.description: Starting May 19, 2026, diesel and gasoline pump prices in the Philippines will increase, while kerosene prices will decrease, according to the Department of Energy (DOE). Diesel prices will rise by ap...

item.title: Caritas Philippines Calls for Communal Prayers as Vice President Sara Duterte Faces Impeachment Trial
item.description: Caritas Philippines, the social aid and development arm of the Catholic Church, has called on Filipino Catholics to engage in communal prayers in connection with the impeachment trial of Vice Presiden...

item.title: TECNO SPARK 50: Smartphone with Long-Lasting Battery and Durable Design for Everyday Use
item.description: Life dey move fast these days, and smartphone wey no fit keep up fit stress person. TECNO don launch their new phone, the SPARK 50, wey dey designed to handle all your daily wahala. E get correct 6700...

item.title: Doctors Without Borders scales up response to Ebola outbreak in the DRC amid regional concerns
item.description: Doctors Without Borders (MSF) has raised serious concerns over a new Ebola outbreak in the Ituri province of the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), with over 80 deaths and 246 suspected cases reporte...

item.title: Delhi High Court Grants Interim Bail to Baramulla MP Engineer Rashid After Father’s Demise
item.description: The Delhi High Court has granted interim bail to Baramulla Member of Parliament, Sheikh Abdul Rashid, also known as Engineer Rashid, until 2nd June 2026 following the demise of his father. Rashid, who...

item.title: James Corden Reflects on a Less Successful Episode of ‘Gavin & Stacey’
item.description: During a recent appearance at Liverpool’s BBC Comedy Festival, James Corden, co-creator and star of the beloved sitcom Gavin & Stacey, spoke candidly about a particular episode from the first series. ...

item.title: Disabled Kenyans Face Severe Challenges During Fuel Price Protests and School Shutdowns
item.description: During the recent nationwide protests over rising fuel prices in Kenya, disabled Kenyans have faced disproportionate difficulties, according to Hafid Maalim Ibrahim, chairman of Persons with Disabilit...

item.title: Kairuki supervises 40 billion/- financing to support MSMEs through NBC-BII
item.description: On May 18, 2026, Tanzanian Minister Kairuki took an active role in overseeing a significant financing deal worth 40 billion Tanzanian shillings, aimed at supporting micro, small, and medium-sized ente...

item.title: Social Media Ban Leads Australian Teens to Access Less News
item.description: Research indicates that the social media ban implemented in Australia in December 2025 is having a notable impact on teenagers' access to news. Prior to the ban, social media was a primary source of n...

item.title: Wichita’s Garlic Salad: A Local Dish Deserving Broader Recognition
item.description: Wichita, Kansas, may not be widely recognized for its culinary contributions, but one local dish—the garlic salad—stands out as a unique regional specialty. Despite its bold flavors and refreshing qua...

item.title: Ottawa’s Canadian Tulip Festival Concludes 74th Annual Celebration
item.description: The Canadian Tulip Festival in Ottawa is concluding its 74th edition this year, marking over seven decades of vibrant spring celebrations. The festival, renowned for its stunning displays of tulips ac...

item.title: Grafana Labs Confirms Code Theft but Refuses to Pay Ransom, No Customer Data Compromised
item.description: Grafana Labs, the company behind the widely used open-source web visualization software, reported a security breach in which hackers accessed its GitLab environment using a stolen token credential. Wh...

item.title: States Propose Holding Oil Companies Accountable for Rising Property Insurance Costs
item.description: As property insurance premiums soar due to increasing natural disasters, some U.S. states are exploring legislation to hold oil companies responsible for climate-related damages. In California, Hawaii...

item.title: MV Hondius to dock in Rotterdam; 27 crew and medical staff to undergo quarantine
item.description: The MV Hondius, a luxury cruise ship flagged in the Netherlands, is set to dock in Rotterdam following an outbreak of hantavirus on board. Dutch authorities have arranged quarantine facilities for the...
